tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58383,"title":"[Azure Generator] Docs: Document auto-scaffolding of README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and Directory.Build.props","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp` (Azure Data Plane Generator) **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/` **Triggered by:** PR #57653 (commit fe4c0e1e) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #57653 updated `NewAzureProjectScaffolding.WriteAdditionalFiles` to automatically generate three scaffolding files when code is first emitted into a directory under `sdk/`: - `README.md` — pre-filled with standard section headers (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) - `CHANGELOG.md` — pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template - `Directory.Build.props` — pre-filled with the standard import chain for azure-sdk-for-net packages Files are only written if they do **not** already exist, so customised content is never overwritten. ## Gaps Found - The emitter README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` has no mention of these auto-generated files or when they are produced - The `new-project` option description only says it overwrites the `.csproj`; there is no explanation that new scaffolding files (README, CHANGELOG, Directory.Build.props) are also created on first use under `sdk/` - There is no note that the scaffolding is intentionally a one-shot operation (files are not overwritten on subsequent regenerations), which is important for users who need to know they can safely customise these files - No guidance on what the generated templates look like or how to extend them via the `GetReadmeContent` / `GetChangelogContent` / `GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent` virtual methods for custom generators 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` **Action:** Edit — add a new sub-section under `## Emitter options` (after the `new-project` option entry) and a new top-level section for scaffolding behaviour. Add the following after the `### new-project` option block: ````markdown ### Scaffolded files (new projects under `sdk/`) When generating a **new** project whose output directory is located under the `sdk/` directory of the azure-sdk-for-net repository, the generator automatically creates the following files alongside the `.csproj` and `.sln` files — **only if they do not already exist**: | File | Description | |---|---| | `README.md` | Pre-filled with standard azure-sdk-for-net section headings (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) | | `CHANGELOG.md` | Pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template | | `Directory.Build.props` | Pre-filled with the standard MSBuild import chain required by azure-sdk-for-net | Because these files are written only once, you can freely customise them after they are generated — subsequent runs of the generator will never overwrite your changes. > **Note:** This scaffolding is only triggered when the emitter output directory contains `/sdk/` in its path. Projects outside `sdk/` (for example, spector test projects under `eng/`) do not receive these files. #### Extending scaffolded content in a custom generator If you have built a custom generator that extends `NewAzureProjectScaffolding`, you can override the following virtual methods to change the generated template content: ```csharp protected virtual string GetReadmeContent(string packageName) { ... } protected virtual string GetChangelogContent(string packageName) { ... } protected virtual string GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent(string packageName) { ... } ``` ```` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation change. The feature is purely generator infrastructure and does not expose a user-facing API surface that would benefit from a runnable sample in a test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The existing README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` already contains all required sections. Only the **Emitter options** section needs the new content described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/Azure.Generator.csproj` — verify the generator still builds 2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/test/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` — verify tests pass (the new `WriteAdditionalFilesTests.cs` should be included) No snippet update or API export steps are required because only documentation (Markdown) is being changed. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24664599157/agentic_workflow) · ● 418.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58383","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T11:49:44Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T11:49:44Z"}

Projects outside `sdk/` (for example, spector test projects under `eng/`) do not receive these files. #### Extending scaffolded content in a custom generator If you have built a custom generator that extends `NewAzureProjectScaffolding`, you can override the following virtual methods to change the generated template content: ```csharp protected virtual string GetReadmeContent(string packageName) { ... } protected virtual string GetChangelogContent(string packageName) { ... } protected virtual string GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent(string packageName) { ... } ``` ```` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation change. The feature is purely generator infrastructure and does not expose a user-facing API surface that would benefit from a runnable sample in a test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The existing README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` already contains all required sections. Only the **Emitter options** section needs the new content described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/Azure.Generator.csproj` — verify the generator still builds 2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/test/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` — verify tests pass (the new `WriteAdditionalFilesTests.cs` should be included) No snippet update or API export steps are required because only documentation (Markdown) is being changed. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24664599157/agentic_workflow) · ● 418.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58383","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T11:49:44Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T11:49:44Z"}

"pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24664599157/agentic_workflow) · ● 418.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58383","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T11:49:44Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T11:49:44Z"}

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** Bot-filed documentation gap issue requesting updates to the Azure Data Plane Generator README to document the auto-scaffolding behavior (README.md, CHANGELOG.md, Directory.Build.props) introduced in PR #57653.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp` (Azure Data Plane Generator, `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/`)
**Affected API:** `NewAzureProjectScaffolding.WriteAdditionalFiles`; virtual methods `GetReadmeContent`, `GetChangelogContent`, `GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent`
**Scenarios:**
- Generator README has no mention of auto-scaffolded files or when they are produced
